# game

> An ECS game framework for Moku — Spark-style public API and memory layout, PixiJS v8 rendering, built on @moku-labs/core, composable with @moku-labs/web, with a first-class MCP plugin exposing the live runtime to agent clients.

`game` is a typed Entity-Component-System runtime composed of eleven plugins. It is unpublished
(version 0.0.0) and not a game engine UI or editor — it owns the data model (archetype object-SoA
storage), the fixed-timestep frame, and the GPU lifecycle; you write components and systems in
TypeScript. Hot-path frame work bypasses the Moku kernel for performance and is NOT emitted as
kernel events. Systems reach shared services (assets, logging, frame time) through a typed world
**resource registry** — the system signature stays `(world, dt)`.

## Core Concepts

- **Three-layer Moku model.** `createCoreConfig` (Layer 1: config + events) → `createCore`
  (Layer 2: framework + the 10 built-in plugins) → `createApp({ pluginConfigs })` (Layer 3: your
  app). Consumers import `createApp` / `createPlugin` from `game` and never import
  `@moku-labs/core` directly. `createApp(...)` returns an App synchronously; `await app.start()`
  boots the Pixi Application and registers DOM listeners. Each plugin's API is reached as
  `app.<plugin>.<method>()` (e.g. `app.ecs`, `app.scheduler`, `app.renderer`, `app.context`).
  **Advanced / headless escape hatches** (also exported from `game`): `createCore(carrier, { plugins })`
  assembles a custom core from a plugin subset (carrier is type-only — pass `{ createPlugin }` or
  `coreConfig`); `createCoreConfig(name, { config, plugins })` (re-exported from `@moku-labs/core`)
  builds a bespoke Layer-1 config with its own `Config`/`Events`/core-plugin set. `createApp` stays
  the default — these are for tooling, tests, and agent-only hosts.
- **Config composition.** The framework defines no global config fields (`Config = {}`). All
  configuration is per-plugin via `createApp({ pluginConfigs: { <plugin>: { ... } } })`. Core
  plugins `logPlugin` + `envPlugin` from `@moku-labs/common` are registered in `config.ts`, so
  `ctx.log` and `ctx.env` are on every plugin context.
- **ECS data model.** `app.ecs` returns the `World` facade directly (no wrapper). Components are
  registered with `defineComponent(create, opts?)` and are themselves callable: `Position({x,y})`
  produces a `ComponentInit` spawn payload. `opts.name` makes a component discoverable by name via
  the introspection facet. Storage is archetype object-SoA by default (`"archetype"`) with a
  per-component `"sparse"` opt-out for high-churn tags. `Entity` is a generational handle
  (`(generation << 16) | index`), so stale handles are detectably dead via `isAlive`. Typed
  variadic `query(...)` covers arities 1–8. Structural mutations during iteration go through a
  deferred command buffer flushed at each stage boundary.
- **ECS introspection facet (read-only, for tooling such as mcp).** `liveEntities()`,
  `entityCount()`, `componentNames()`, `componentsOf(entity)`, and `componentByName(name)` expose
  the live world without touching layout. `componentByName` resolves a component registered with
  `opts.name` to its callable token (widened to `Component<Record<string, unknown>>` so callers
  pass partial values without per-component generics); `undefined` for unknown/anonymous names,
  first-wins on duplicate names.
- **World resources (first-class systems access).** The world owns a typed singleton registry —
  `Resource<T>` tokens identify shared services/state systems read through the `world` argument
  with no closure capture. `defineResource(create?)` mints an auto-keyed (`"res:N"`) token and
  registers an optional lazy factory; `setResource`/`getResource`/`resource`/`hasResource`/
  `removeResource` read and write. `resource(token)` asserts presence (THROWS an actionable error
  if unset with no factory); `getResource(token)` returns `T | undefined`. Resource ops are
  **immediate** — they bypass the command buffer even mid-iteration, and are NOT counted by
  `maxStructuralOpsWarn`. Framework well-known resources are wired at `app.start()`: `Assets` +
  `GameContext` (context plugin), `Time` (loop plugin).
- **Fixed-timestep loop.** The `loop` plugin owns the frame: a `requestAnimationFrame` loop with a
  fixed-timestep accumulator. Each frame it clamps the real delta (`maxFrameDelta`), runs
  `scheduler.tick(fixedDt)` zero-or-more times (capped at `maxStepsPerFrame`), then calls
  `renderer.render()` once. `step()` advances exactly one deterministic tick + render and now
  RETURNS a `TimeStepResult` (`{ frame, elapsed, dt }`) snapshot of the just-advanced clock
  (`{ 0, 0, 0 }` before start / after stop; widening from `void` is non-breaking). Each fixed step
  it updates the `Time` world resource in place (`{ dt, elapsed, frame }`, seconds; no per-step
  allocation).
- **Scheduler stages.** Five fixed, ordered stages: `input → update → physics → sync → render`.
  Systems are `(world, dt) => void`. The command buffer is flushed between stages. `scheduler` is a
  thin facade that forwards to `world.addSystem` / `world.tick`.
- **Polled input + injection.** DOM listeners mutate a live resource; an `"input"`-stage system
  freezes it into an immutable `InputSnapshot` per frame. Programmatic `keyDown`/`keyUp`/`keyPress`
  inject keys (for agents/tests); each normalizes a small alias set first — `"Space"`/`"Spacebar"`
  → `" "`, `"Esc"` → `"Escape"`, all other keys pass through — so the snapshot observes the
  canonical `KeyboardEvent.key`.
- **MCP runtime exposure.** The `mcp` plugin attaches a Model Context Protocol server exposing
  read-only + mutating tools and JSON resources (15 tools total). The transport default is
  **environment-aware**: `["stdio"]` under Node/Bun, `["inMemory"]` in a browser (an in-page
  `InMemoryTransport` pair, no socket — reach the client side via `app.mcp.clientTransport()`);
  Streamable HTTP is opt-in. The stdio transport is guarded on `process?.stdin` so a browser
  default never crashes. Mutating tools enqueue closures drained on the next `"input"` stage tick
  (frame-safe). Mutations are honest: unknown component/scene names, dead entities, and headless
  renderers return MCP `isError` / `{ changed: false }` rather than optimistic success.
- **Headless renderer.** `renderer.headless` (auto-detected: `true` when `typeof document ===
  "undefined"`, explicitly overridable) skips Pixi/GPU entirely in `onStart` — no `Application` is
  created, `state.app` stays `undefined`, `render()`/`getView()`/`getStage()`/`screenshot()`/
  `tree()`/`attachPrimitive()` become safe no-ops/`undefined`/`false` — but `Transform` is still
  defined and the `sync` system still registered, so ECS/scene code is identical headless or not.

## Quick Reference

```ts
import { createApp, createPlugin } from "game";

const app = createApp({
  pluginConfigs: {
    ecs:       { initialCapacity: 1024, maxStructuralOpsWarn: 0 },
    scheduler: { strictStages: true },
    renderer:  { width: 800, height: 600, background: 0x000000, antialias: true, mount: "#game" },
    input:     { target: "window", keyboard: true, pointer: true, preventDefault: false },
    loop:      { fixedDt: 1 / 60, maxFrameDelta: 0.25, maxStepsPerFrame: 5, autoStart: true },
    assets:    { basePath: "assets/", manifest: { ship: "sprites/ship.png" }, throwOnError: true },
    context:   { bindGameContext: true },
    scene:     { despawnOnUnload: true },
    mcp:       { transports: ["stdio"], httpPort: 3333, httpAuth: "none", enableMutations: true }
  }
});
await app.start();          // async — boots Pixi Application, attaches DOM listeners, binds resources
// ... use the app ...
await app.stop();           // tears down GPU + listeners
```

### app.ecs — the World facade (Complex)

```ts
defineComponent<T>(create: () => T, opts?: { storage?: "archetype" | "sparse"; name?: string }): Component<T>
defineTag(opts?: { storage?: "archetype" | "sparse"; name?: string }): Tag      // storage default "sparse"
// component tokens are callable: Position({ x, y }) => ComponentInit
spawn(...parts: ComponentInit[]): Entity
despawn(entity: Entity): void
isAlive(entity: Entity): boolean
add<T>(entity, component, value?: Partial<T>): void
remove<T>(entity, component): void
has<T>(entity, component): boolean
get<T>(entity, component): T | undefined
set<T>(entity, component, value: Partial<T>): void
query(...components): Query<Values>            // overloads arity 1..8
//   Query: updateEach(cb), count(), first(), [Symbol.iterator]
addSystem(stage: Stage, system: System): () => void
tick(dt: number): void                         // runs all stages in order, flushes buffer between
// ── Introspection (read-only — for tooling such as the mcp plugin) ──
liveEntities(): readonly Entity[]              // fresh snapshot of all live entity handles
entityCount(): number                          // direct count of live entities
componentNames(): readonly string[]            // names of components defined with opts.name (registration order)
componentsOf(entity): ReadonlyArray<{ name: string; value: unknown }>  // named components on an entity
componentByName(name: string): Component<Record<string, unknown>> | undefined  // name → callable token; first-wins on dup
// ── World resources (typed singletons) — immediate, never command-buffered ──
defineResource<T>(create?: () => T): Resource<T>   // mints "res:N" key + optional lazy factory
setResource<T>(resource: Resource<T>, value: T): void
getResource<T>(resource: Resource<T>): T | undefined  // lazy-init from factory; undefined if unset+no factory
resource<T>(resource: Resource<T>): T              // same lazy-init, THROWS if unset + no factory
hasResource<T>(resource: Resource<T>): boolean     // true if a value is set OR a factory exists
removeResource<T>(resource: Resource<T>): void     // clears value; factory (if any) re-inits next read
// Resource<T> = { readonly __key: string; readonly __value?: T }  // __value is phantom (type-level only)
```

### app.scheduler (Standard)

```ts
readonly stages: readonly Stage[]              // ["input","update","physics","sync","render"]
addSystem(stage: Stage, system: System): () => void   // forwards to world.addSystem
tick(dt: number): void                                // forwards to world.tick
```

### app.renderer (Complex) — PixiJS v8 (headless-capable)

```ts
readonly Transform: Component<{ x; y; rotation; scaleX; scaleY }>   // throws if read before start(); defined even when headless
attach(entity: Entity, view: Container): void  // Container/Sprite/Graphics — caller owns stage.addChild
attachPrimitive(entity: Entity, spec: PrimitiveSpec): boolean  // builds a Pixi Graphics from plain data, ADDS it to the stage; false when headless/before start
detach(entity: Entity): void                   // destroy()s view, idempotent
render(): void                                 // draws one frame; no-op before start AND when headless
screenshot(): Promise<string | undefined>      // PNG data URL via Pixi extract; undefined when headless/before start
tree(): SceneNode | undefined                  // JSON scene-graph snapshot; SceneNode.type ∈ Text|Graphics|Sprite|Container
getView(): HTMLCanvasElement | undefined       // canvas for manual mounting; undefined when headless
getStage(): Container | undefined              // root Pixi stage; undefined when headless
markDirty(entity: Entity): void                // re-sync view next "sync" tick
// PrimitiveStyle = { fill?: number; stroke?: number; strokeWidth?: number; alpha?: number; label?: string }  // hex-int colors, no Pixi types
// PrimitiveSpec = discriminated union on `shape`:
//   { shape:"rect"; width; height } | { shape:"circle"; radius }
//   | { shape:"line"; x2; y2 } | { shape:"polygon"; points: ReadonlyArray<{x;y}> }  (each & PrimitiveStyle)
//   Anchor: rect & circle are CENTERED on the entity's Transform; polygon points are local (origin at Transform); line runs from the origin to (x2,y2).
// Config.headless: boolean — @default detectHeadless() (true when typeof document === "undefined").
```

### app.input (Standard) — polled + injectable

```ts
snapshot(): InputSnapshot
keyDown(key: string): void    // hold a key (alias-normalized: "Space"/"Spacebar"→" ", "Esc"→"Escape")
keyUp(key: string): void      // release a key (alias-normalized)
keyPress(key: string): void   // one-frame tap: justPressed + justReleased, never sticks (alias-normalized)
// InputSnapshot:
//   isDown(key: string): boolean
//   justPressed(key: string): boolean         // one frame only
//   justReleased(key: string): boolean         // one frame only
//   readonly pointer: { x: number; y: number; buttons: number }  // buttons: 1=L 2=R 4=M
// Injection is immediate (between frames, NOT command-buffered); the snapshot observes the canonical key.
```

### app.loop (Standard) — fixed-timestep rAF + Time resource

```ts
start(): void          // begin/resume rAF loop (no-op if running)
stop(): void           // pause; cancels pending frame
isRunning(): boolean
step(): TimeStepResult // one deterministic fixed tick + render; returns { frame, elapsed, dt }; { 0,0,0 } before start/after stop
readonly time: Resource<TimeState>   // well-known Time token, also exported as Time from loop/resources
// TimeState (all readonly to consumers; loop mutates in place each step — no allocation):
//   dt: number        // fixed step length in SECONDS (== config.fixedDt)
//   elapsed: number   // total simulated time in SECONDS (sum of all fixed steps)
//   frame: number     // count of fixed steps since start (not seconds)
// TimeStepResult = Pick<TimeState, "frame" | "elapsed" | "dt">
// read from a system: world.resource(app.loop.time) → { dt, elapsed, frame }
```

### app.assets (Standard) — Pixi v8 Assets wrapper

```ts
load(alias: string): Promise<Texture>                                   // emits assets:loaded {kind:"asset"}
loadBundle(bundle: string, entries: Record<string,string>): Promise<Record<string,Texture>> // emits {kind:"bundle"}
get(alias: string): Texture | undefined                                 // cache read, no load
sprite(alias: string): Promise<Sprite>                                  // load-on-miss, build Sprite
isLoaded(alias: string): boolean                                        // session state, not Pixi cache
```

### app.context (Standard) — well-known world resources

```ts
readonly assets: Resource<AssetsApi>           // __key "ctx:assets"; ALWAYS bound at onStart
readonly game:   Resource<GameContextValue>    // __key "ctx:game"; bound only when bindGameContext (default true)
// Both tokens are fixed-key module consts (valid before start); onStart binds their VALUES on the world.
// GameContextValue = { readonly log: LogApi; readonly emit: EmitFn<FrameworkEvents>; readonly env: EnvApi }
// read from a system: world.resource(app.context.assets) / world.resource(app.context.game)
```

### app.scene (Standard) — named lifecycle + ownership

```ts
define(name: string, definition: SceneDefinition): void
//   SceneDefinition = { setup: (world: World) => void | Promise<void>; bundle?: Record<string,string> }
load(name: string): Promise<void>     // unload current → preload bundle → run setup → emit scene:loaded
unload(): void                        // detach + despawn owned entities (if despawnOnUnload)
currentScene(): string | undefined
sceneNames(): readonly string[]       // registered scene names (registration order); [] before any define
ownedEntities(): readonly Entity[]    // snapshot of current scene's owned entities; [] after unload
```

### app.mcp (Complex) — MCP server (env-aware transports)

```ts
isRunning(): boolean
httpEndpoint(): string | undefined    // e.g. "http://127.0.0.1:3333/mcp", or undefined when no "http" transport
toolNames(): readonly string[]
clientTransport(): InMemoryClientTransportLike | undefined  // in-page client paired with the server when "inMemory" is active
// Config.transports: ReadonlyArray<"stdio" | "http" | "inMemory">
//   @default env-aware: ["inMemory"] in a browser, ["stdio"] under Node/Bun. "inMemory" is in-page only (no socket).
// Config.enableMutations: boolean — @default true. false → only the 4 read-only tools are registered.
// Config.inMemoryGlobalKey: string — @default "__MOKU_GAME_MCP__" ("" disables the globalThis publish).
```

MCP tools — 15 total.
Read-only (always, 4): `ecs:query`, `renderer:screenshot`, `renderer:tree`, `scene:getInfo`.
Mutating / play (when `enableMutations: true`, 11): `ecs:spawn` (REAL components resolved via
`componentByName`), `ecs:despawn`, `ecs:setComponent`, `ecs:removeComponent`, `renderer:attach`
(visible primitive), `input:key`, `loop:step`, `loop:pause`, `loop:resume`, `scene:load`,
`game:reset`. Unknown component/scene names + dead entities return `isError`; despawn/remove report
`{ changed: boolean }`.
MCP resources: `game://world/snapshot` (every live entity + named values), `game://systems/list`
(stage names only — systems are anonymous), `game://stats/frame`, `game://scene/current`.

### app.audio (Standard) — native WebAudio SFX + music (zero deps)

```ts
unlock(): Promise<void>                          // resume AudioContext after a user gesture; idempotent; sets state.unlocked
load(name: string, url?: string): Promise<void>  // fetch + decodeAudioData + cache; url falls back to manifest[name]; one decode per name
play(name: string, opts?: { volume?: number; rate?: number }): void       // one-shot on sfx; no-op if headless/not-unlocked/not-loaded
playMusic(name: string, opts?: { loop?: boolean; fadeIn?: number }): void // looping on music; stops previous; fadeIn ramps from 0
stopMusic(opts?: { fadeOut?: number }): void     // stop current track; fadeOut ramps to 0 first
mute(): void                                     // zero the master mute bus; emits audio:muteChanged on change
unmute(): void                                   // restore master gain to masterVolume; emits on change
setMuted(muted: boolean): void                   // explicit; emits only on change
isMuted(): boolean
setVolume(channel: Channel, value: number): void // clamp 0..1; write gain; emit audio:volumeChanged on change; master-while-muted keeps bus at 0
getVolume(channel: Channel): number
// Channel = "master" | "sfx" | "music".
// Gain graph: source → channelGain(sfx|music) → masterGain(mute bus) → destination. Held in a ctx.global WeakMap.
// Config: masterVolume/sfxVolume/musicVolume (0..1, @default 1), muted (@default false), manifest (name→url, @default {}).
//   Volume/mute config double as rehydration inputs a storage/platform plugin feeds via pluginConfigs.audio.*.
// Headless-safe: with no AudioContext (SSR/tests) every method no-ops (getters still return state). onStart/onStop own the AudioContext.
```

### app.storage (Standard) — versioned key/value save persistence (zero deps)

```ts
get<T>(key: string, fallback?: T): T | undefined  // JSON-parse; fallback on missing/unparseable/unavailable; triggers lazy migration; never throws
set(key: string, value: unknown): boolean         // JSON-serialize + write; false on rejected write / unserializable value; never throws
has(key: string): boolean
remove(key: string): void                          // no-op if absent
clear(): void                                      // wipe every namespace key, then re-stamp the schema version
keys(): string[]                                   // namespace keys, `${namespace}:` prefix stripped, meta key excluded; [] on failure
isPersistent(): boolean                            // false on the in-memory fallback / an injected non-persistent backend
getVersion(): number                               // schema version in effect after any lazy migration
setBackend(backend: StorageBackend): void          // inject platform's CrazyGames backend; resets `migrated` → re-migrate on next access
// Values stored as `${namespace}:${key}` (JSON). StorageBackend = { getItem→string|null, setItem→boolean, removeItem, keys(prefix)→string[], persistent } — synchronous, non-throwing.
// Config: namespace (@default "game"), version (@default 1), migrations (target-version → whole-namespace Snapshot transform, @default {}).
// Migration runs lazily on first access, once (memoized): migrations[n] upgrades a v(n-1) snapshot to vn; a store already at version is untouched; a downgrade warns + leaves data intact.
// Safe by construction: the default backend probes localStorage and falls back to an in-memory Map (persistent=false) when partitioned/blocked/absent — get/set never crash the game. No events, no hooks, no lifecycle.
```

## Events

The catalog is intentionally tiny — per-frame work is on the hot path, off the event bus.

- `assets:loaded` — `{ alias: string; kind: "asset" | "bundle" }` — after `load()`/`loadBundle()`. (framework Events)
- `scene:loaded` — `{ name: string }` — after a scene's `setup` completes during `load()`. (framework Events)
- `game:reset` — `{ reason: "mcp" }` — after the mcp `game:reset` tool despawns all MCP-tracked
  entities and unloads the current scene. (mcp plugin event — declared + emitted by the mcp plugin)
- `audio:muteChanged` — `{ muted: boolean }` — after `mute()`/`unmute()`/`setMuted()` changes the
  mute state. (audio plugin event — a storage/platform plugin persists it)
- `audio:volumeChanged` — `{ channel: "master" | "sfx" | "music"; value: number }` — after
  `setVolume()` changes a channel volume. (audio plugin event)

`assets:loaded`/`scene:loaded` are declared in `src/config.ts` (framework `Events`); `game:reset`
(mcp) and `audio:muteChanged`/`audio:volumeChanged` (audio) are declared on their plugins' `Events`.
Subscribe from a consumer plugin's `hooks`. The `context` plugin exposes the framework emitter to
systems via `GameContext.emit`, but owns no events of its own.

## File Structure

```
game/
├── src/
│   ├── config.ts            # Layer 1: createCoreConfig, Config = {}, Events, createPlugin/createCore
│   ├── index.ts             # Layer 2: createCore assembles the 11 plugins; exports createApp/createPlugin (+ createCore/createCoreConfig for advanced/headless assembly)
│   └── plugins/
│       ├── index.ts         # plugin barrel (instances + type namespaces)
│       ├── ecs/             # data/runtime core + introspection + resource registry (world.ts, types.ts, README.md, __tests__/)
│       ├── scheduler/       # stage contract facade
│       ├── renderer/        # PixiJS v8 backend (api.ts, lifecycle.ts, sync.ts, primitives.ts, types.ts)
│       ├── input/           # polled keyboard/pointer + key injection (api.ts)
│       ├── loop/            # fixed-timestep rAF loop + Time resource (resources.ts)
│       ├── assets/          # Pixi Assets wrapper
│       ├── context/         # binds Assets + GameContext world resources (resources.ts, lifecycle.ts)
│       ├── scene/           # named scene lifecycle + ownership
│       ├── mcp/             # MCP server (transport.ts, tools.ts, resources.ts)
│       ├── audio/           # native WebAudio SFX + music, mute bus (engine.ts, lifecycle.ts)
│       └── storage/         # versioned key/value save persistence (backend.ts, migrate.ts)
├── tests/                   # framework-level unit + integration tests
├── .planning/specs/         # design specs (intent)
├── README.md
├── llms.txt
└── llms-full.txt
```

## Plugins

Built/resolved in dependency order. (depends → resolved with `ctx.require`.)

- **ecs** — data/runtime core: generational entities, archetype object-SoA storage, typed queries,
  deferred command buffer, read-only introspection facet, typed world resource registry,
  `world.tick`. | Complex | `defineComponent`/`spawn`/`query`/`addSystem`/`liveEntities`/
  `entityCount`/`componentNames`/`componentsOf`/`componentByName`/`defineResource`/`setResource`/
  `getResource`/`resource`/`hasResource`/`removeResource`/`tick` | events: none | depends: none.
- **scheduler** — ordered stage contract; thin facade over the world. | Standard |
  `addSystem(stage,fn)`/`tick`/`stages` | events: none | depends: ecs.
- **renderer** — PixiJS v8 backend; owns the GPU `Application`, defines `Transform`, syncs
  ECS→display objects on the `sync` stage, attaches plain-data primitives. First-class `headless`
  config (auto-detected; skips Pixi/GPU, still defines `Transform` + sync). | Complex |
  `Transform`/`attach`/`attachPrimitive`/`detach`/`render`/`screenshot`/`tree`/`getView`/`getStage`/
  `markDirty` | new types: `PrimitiveSpec`, `PrimitiveStyle`, `SceneNode` | config: +`headless` |
  events: none | depends: ecs, scheduler.
- **input** — polled keyboard/pointer from DOM listeners → per-frame `InputSnapshot` on the `input`
  stage; programmatic `keyDown`/`keyUp`/`keyPress` injection with alias normalization
  (`"Space"`/`"Spacebar"`→`" "`, `"Esc"`→`"Escape"`). | Standard |
  `snapshot`/`keyDown`/`keyUp`/`keyPress` | events: none | depends: scheduler.
- **loop** — fixed-timestep rAF loop driving `scheduler.tick` then `renderer.render` each frame
  (kernel-bypassing hot path); publishes + mutates the `Time` world resource in place each step;
  `step()` returns a `TimeStepResult` clock snapshot. | Standard | `start`/`stop`/`step`/
  `isRunning`/`time` | new type: `TimeStepResult` | events: none | depends: scheduler, renderer, ecs.
- **assets** — Pixi v8 `Assets` wrapper: load/cache textures + bundles, build sprites. | Standard |
  `load`/`loadBundle`/`sprite`/`get`/`isLoaded` | events: `assets:loaded` | depends: renderer.
- **context** — binds the well-known `Assets` + `GameContext` world resources at start so systems
  reach framework services via `world.resource(token)`; static API exposes the two tokens. |
  Standard | `app.context` → `assets`/`game` | events: none | depends: ecs, assets.
- **scene** — named scene lifecycle with entity-ownership tracking, clean transitions, bundle
  pre-load. | Standard | `define`/`load`/`unload`/`currentScene`/`sceneNames`/`ownedEntities` |
  events: `scene:loaded` | depends: ecs, renderer, assets.
- **mcp** — first-class MCP server exposing the runtime to agents over stdio / Streamable HTTP /
  in-page `inMemory` (env-aware default: `["stdio"]` Node-Bun, `["inMemory"]` browser; stdio guarded
  on `process?.stdin`). 15 tools (4 read-only; 11 mutation/play gated by `enableMutations`). |
  Complex | `isRunning`/`httpEndpoint`/`toolNames`/`clientTransport` | config: +`inMemoryGlobalKey` |
  events: `game:reset` (`{ reason: "mcp" }`) | depends: ecs, scheduler, renderer, input, loop, assets, scene.
- **audio** — native WebAudio SFX + music (zero runtime deps, no Howler/Pixi). Owns a small gain
  graph `source → channelGain(sfx|music) → masterGain → destination` whose `masterGain` is the
  **mute bus** a future `platform` adapter ducks on ad breaks. Pooled one-shots (decoded-buffer
  cache), looping music with fade, per-channel + master volume, user-gesture `unlock()` (no
  autoplay). Headless-safe (every method no-ops without an `AudioContext`); owns the `AudioContext`
  via onStart/onStop. | Standard | `unlock`/`load`/`play`/`playMusic`/`stopMusic`/`mute`/`unmute`/
  `setMuted`/`isMuted`/`setVolume`/`getVolume` | new types: `Channel`, `PlayOptions`,
  `PlayMusicOptions`, `StopMusicOptions` | events: `audio:muteChanged`, `audio:volumeChanged` |
  depends: none (foundational, Wave 1).
- **storage** — namespaced, versioned key/value save persistence with a migration chain behind a
  pluggable synchronous `StorageBackend` seam. Safe default: probes `localStorage`, falls back to an
  in-memory `Map` (partitioned/blocked/absent) — get/set never throw. Migration runs lazily on first
  access, once (memoized); `setBackend()` (the future `platform` CrazyGames adapter) resets it. No
  events, no hooks, no lifecycle. | Standard | `get`/`set`/`has`/`remove`/`clear`/`keys`/
  `isPersistent`/`getVersion`/`setBackend` | new types: `StorageBackend`, `Migration`, `Snapshot` |
  config: `namespace`/`version`/`migrations` | events: none | depends: none (foundational, Wave 1).

## Requirements

- Node >= 24, Bun >= 1.3.14 (use bun exclusively). TypeScript strict +
  `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` + `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`.
- Dependencies: `@moku-labs/core`, `@moku-labs/common`, `pixi.js@^8`. Composable with
  `@moku-labs/web`. mcp adds `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk`, `hono`, `zod`.

## Scripts

`bun run build` (tsdown) · `bun run lint` (biome + eslint) · `bun run lint:fix` ·
`bun run format` · `bun run test` (vitest run) · `bun run test:unit` ·
`bun run test:integration` · `bun run test:coverage`.
